title: How to File Consumer Complaint Online — National Commission Courts
meta_description: Consumer complaint online 2026 — edaakhil.nic.in portal, 3 tiers of consumer courts, process, fees ₹0-5000, timeline, and compensation guide.

hero.one_liner: File complaint against companies, hospitals, builders, insurers at edaakhil.nic.in — consumer court is faster than regular courts and awards compensation

sections.heading: What Counts as Consumer Complaint?
content: Valid Complaints: (1) Defective products (mobile, TV, car, appliance that breaks, malfunctions, or doesn't work as advertised), (2) Poor services (hospital medical negligence, bank wrong charges, insurance claim rejection without valid reason, builder not completing construction on time), (3) Unfair trade practices (false advertising, hidden charges not disclosed at time of purchase, misleading product claims), (4) E-commerce issues (wrong product delivered, seller/platform refuses refund, counterfeit goods, order cancelled without reason).

NOT Consumer Complaints: (1) Pure contractual disputes (business-to-business contracts), (2) Service disputes that are already covered under separate regulation (like stock market disputes — go to stock exchange grievance), (3) Allegations of criminal acts (like fraud — go to police instead, though you can file both).
Examples of Successful Complaints: Mobile phone declared 'new' but delivered used → ₹50,000 compensation. Hospital surgery without proper consent → ₹5,00,000 compensation.
Insurance claim wrongly rejected → Full claim amount + 10% penalty. Builder delayed possession by 2 years → ₹20 lakh compensation for mental agony + full refund.

sections.heading: How to File Complaint Online — Step by Step

steps.title: Visit edaakhil.nic.in — Electronic Filing Portal
steps.description: Go to the National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission portal. This is the central portal for filing complaints at all three commission levels. Click 'New User' → Create account with email and mobile number.

steps.title: Login and select 'File a Consumer Complaint'
steps.description: After email verification and login, click 'File a Consumer Complaint'. Choose: (1) Type of complaint (product defect, service deficiency, unfair trade practice, etc.), (2) Commission level based on claim amount.

steps.title: Choose the correct forum based on claim amount
steps.description: Up to ₹50 lakh → File at your DISTRICT commission (faster, 3-8 months). ₹50 lakh to ₹2 crore → File at STATE commission. Above ₹2 crore → File at NATIONAL commission in Delhi. Each level has a different fee — smaller claims = lower fees.

steps.title: Fill complaint details (Part 1: Your Information)
steps.description: Name, address, email, mobile number, occupation, Aadhaar (optional but recommended for quick processing). Select the correct language of complaint (Hindi/English). Affidavit mode: Digital signature via Aadhaar e-Sign (eases verification).

steps.title: Part 2: Opposite Party (Defendant) Details
steps.description: Company/Hospital/Builder name and full address, contact number if available, type of business (e-commerce, healthcare, real estate, insurance, telecom, etc.). You can file against: the company itself, the individual owner, or authorized representatives — all can be parties.

steps.title: Part 3: Complaint Description
steps.description: What exactly happened? (1) When did you purchase/use the product/service? (2) What was promised vs what you received? (3) What damages did you suffer? Write clearly — judges read thousands of complaints, so be specific. Example: 'On 15-Jan-2025, I purchased a Samsung TV from ABC Electronics for ₹45,000. It worked for 3 days then stopped. Despite 5 service requests, the defect wasn't fixed. I missed work for 10 days due to this and suffered mental agony.' → This is specific.

steps.title: Part 4: Relief Sought
steps.description: What do you want? (1) Refund of amount paid, (2) Replacement of product, (3) Compensation for mental agony and harassment, (4) Litigation cost (court fee), (5) Penalty. Claim amount example: Refund ₹45,000 + compensation ₹1,00,000 + litigation cost ₹10,000 = Total ₹1,55,000. TOTAL AMOUNT CLAIMED determines the forum level.

steps.title: Part 5: Upload Evidence
steps.description: Attach: (1) Purchase receipt/invoice, (2) Product warranty card or service agreement, (3) Communication with company (emails, WhatsApp screenshots, call logs), (4) Photos/videos of defective product, (5) Repair bills or expert's defect report, (6) Medical reports (if medical negligence), (7) Any written complaint letter sent to company earlier, (8) Affidavit on stamp paper (₹10 stamp) stating the facts are true. All files JPEG/PDF, max 2MB each.

steps.title: Pay court fee based on claim amount
steps.description: Zero to ₹1 lakh: ₹100-200. ₹1-10 lakh: ₹300-500. ₹10-50 lakh: ₹1,000-2,000. ₹50 lakh+ varies. Pay online via net banking/UPI. Fee is one-time and non-refundable, but if you win, the court may ask the opposite party to reimburse it.

steps.title: Submit and get complaint number
steps.description: System auto-validates all fields. If correct, complaint is filed instantly. You get a complaint/case number (like 'CC/12345/2026'). Email confirmation is sent with complaint details.

steps.title: Commission schedules hearing dates
steps.description: Within 2-4 weeks, the commission schedules hearing dates. Both parties are sent notices. You'll attend hearings at the commission office on the scheduled date. Bring original documents.

steps.title: Hearing and judgment
steps.description: You present your case (no lawyer necessary for district level). The company presents their defense. Commission examines evidence and issues order within 30 days of final hearing (in theory) — but can take longer. If you win, order is passed within weeks. Comply within 30 days or face contempt action.

sections.heading: Before Filing — Mandatory Pre-Complaint Steps
content: Send Written Complaint First: Before filing in consumer court, send a formal complaint letter to the company via registered post or email. Include: (1) What's wrong, (2) Date of purchase, (3) What relief you seek, (4) Deadline for response (typically 15-30 days).

Keep proof of sending — email delivery receipt or registered post receipt. Why? (1) Courts appreciate that you tried to resolve it first, (2) If company ignores your letter, it strengthens your case, (3) Some companies settle before court to avoid bad publicity.
If they respond positively, you may not need court. If Company Doesn't Respond: After 30 days of non-response to your registered letter, file consumer complaint with that letter as evidence.
If Company Responds But Unsatisfactory: If they deny liability or offer insufficient refund, file court complaint with their response as evidence.

sections.heading: Possible Compensation & Outcomes
rows: 100% of amount paid for the defective product/service
New product in place of defective one
Free repair of the product at company's expense
Extra fine on company (in cases of gross negligence) — ₹10,000-₹2,00,000
Simple interest from date of complaint filing until refund is paid
In cases of severe negligence, courts award everything combined

faqs.question: Do I need a lawyer to file consumer complaint?
faqs.answer: Not mandatory for District Commission. You can argue your own case — many people do and win. However, for complex cases (medical negligence, high-value claims) or State/National Commission, a lawyer helps. Lawyer cost: ₹5,000-20,000 for district-level, ₹20,000-50,000+ for higher commissions. Many consumer rights lawyers offer free initial consultation.

faqs.question: Can I file complaint against Amazon/Flipkart directly?
faqs.answer: YES! The Consumer Protection Act 2019 holds e-commerce platforms responsible. File against BOTH: (1) The e-commerce platform (Amazon/Flipkart), (2) The seller. Collect evidence: order screenshot, delivery confirmation, chat with seller/platform, photos of the product received. E-commerce platforms have started losing many cases — courts hold them liable for fake products and unfulfilled refunds.

faqs.question: How long does a consumer case take to resolve?
faqs.answer: District Commission: 3-8 months average (court target is 3 months, but gets extended). State Commission: 6-12 months. National Commission: 8-15 months. Simple cases (defective phone, clear defect) resolve faster — 2-3 months. Complex cases (medical negligence, builder fraud) take longer — 10-15 months. Once order is passed, company has 30 days to comply or face contempt action.

faqs.question: What if the opposite party is a large company with lawyers?
faqs.answer: Don't worry. Consumer commissions are specifically designed to help common people against big companies. Judges are experienced in handling such cases. In fact, courts often favor the consumer because there's an 'inequality of bargaining power' — the company is stronger than the individual. Many large companies lose cases in consumer court. Your evidence is what matters, not legal expertise.

faqs.question: Can I claim compensation for inconvenience and mental agony even if I get a refund?
faqs.answer: Yes! Refund is separate from compensation. For mental agony, you claim extra compensation depending on: (1) Severity of defect, (2) How long issue persisted, (3) How much you suffered. For example: Phone defect for 1 week = ₹5,000 compensation. Medical negligence causing hospitalization = ₹1,00,000-10,00,000. Courts almost always award mental agony compensation along with refund.

faqs.question: What if the company doesn't comply with the court order?
faqs.answer: File 'Contempt of Commission' petition. The commission can: (1) Fine the company, (2) Seize company assets, (3) Disqualify business (in extreme cases). The commission can also initiate criminal prosecution. Non-compliance with court order is serious and courts take it very seriously.

faqs.question: Can I appeal if I lose the case?
faqs.answer: Yes! If District Commission rejects your complaint, you can appeal to State Commission. If State Commission rejects, appeal to National Commission (highest authority). Each appeal costs a fee (₹2,000-5,000) but if you believe you have a strong case, appeal. Successful appeals happen regularly.

disclaimer: Consumer complaint procedures are as per Consumer Protection Act 2019. edaakhil.nic.in is the official filing portal. States may have specific variations. Always check consumeraffairs.nic.in for latest guidelines and updates.
